_comment: The touchpoint manifest (SPEC INV-205, ROADMAP 413). Every point of contact with the person has a KIND, and the kind decides what may be said there. Each touchpoint declares its kind here, and guardrails/check-touchpoint-kind.py reds a surface that speaks in a kind its touchpoint lacks. 'kind': synchronous = the person is present and the work waits on him; asynchronous = he reads on his own clock while the work rolls. 'opened_by': person = he opens/initiates the contact himself; agent = the agent pushes it at him unbidden. The afforded traffic derives from the two: an interruption is afforded only on a synchronous point; a teaching line only where the person opens the point (any synchronous point, or an asynchronous point he opens on request); waiting traffic is afforded everywhere. 'surface' is the file(s) the touchpoint's output lives in, or null where the surface is not built yet [target]. 'row' cites the roadmap row the touchpoint grounds, where it is one of the frame's four instances.

touchpoints.name: ask-user-question
touchpoints.note: a live question the work waits on; the person is present and answering.

touchpoints.name: decision-page
touchpoints.note: the batched decision page he is looking at now; the work waits on his word.

touchpoints.name: status-report
touchpoints.note: the status line the agent pushes at him; he reads it on his own clock, it interrupts nothing.

touchpoints.name: resume-file
touchpoints.note: the resume file he opens on a cold start; asynchronous, and he opens it himself.

touchpoints.name: inbox-note
touchpoints.note: a note deposited in the inbox; read on his own clock, no interruption, no teaching push.

touchpoints.name: release-note
touchpoints.note: the changelog's offer of next steps; a human-audience, no-answer-needed surface he reads when he opens the release notes himself, so an offer (a teaching-adjacent line) is afforded and an interruption is not.

touchpoints.name: far-tier-surfacing
touchpoints.note: the far backlog's rare self-surfacing; it rides the status report the agent pushes at him, so it may only wait — no interruption, no teaching push.

touchpoints.name: waiting-list
touchpoints.note: the small file at the host root holding what waits for his eyes; he opens it on request, so it affords a teaching line as well as a wait, and never an interruption.

touchpoints.name: parked-feedback-question
touchpoints.note: a question whose value is his input, born onto the waiting list carrying the default the work already took; it rides the waiting list he opens, so it waits for his free minute and never interrupts.

touchpoints.name: decision-readback
touchpoints.note: the decision-set record — what the pack believes the person decided, each entry naming its exchange (SPEC INV-207). He opens it on his own clock and strikes what he never said, so it affords a teaching line and a wait, never an interruption; rendered for him through scripts/render-doc.py.
